title: Handling a Delay at the Airport
conversation: VOICE: Attention passengers on Flight 394 to Chicago, This flight has been delayed. Please stand by for more information. Tom: Excuse me, I'm on the flight to Chicago. When will it depart now? Mike: It looks like it'll be delayed at least two hours. I'll keep you posted. Tom: I have a 4 o'clock meeting in Chicago. If the flight gets in at 3:30, there's no way I'll make it. Mike: Sorry, but there's nothing I can do. There are lots of other people here in the same boat. Tom: Are there any other flights to Chicago? Mike: Yes, there's a 1 p.m. departure, but it's fully booked. Tom: Can you put me on the waiting list? Mike: I'll add you to the list, but don't hold your breath. There are quite a few people already on the list Tom: Can I fly another airline? Mike: Other airlines won't honor your Flyaway Airlines ticket. Tom: Oh, for crying out loud! This is so annoying. Mike: I'm sorry you're annoyed, but there's nothing more I can do. Tom: Let me give you a piece of my mind. I won't be using Flyaway Airlines again anytime soon!

idioms.meaning_en: to wait and be ready for further information or instructions
idioms.meaning_vi: chờ đợi, sẵn sàng đợi lệnh hoặc thông tin tiếp theo
idioms.example: Please stand by while we try to reconnect the video call.
idioms.context: Please stand by for more information.

idioms.meaning_en: to regularly give someone the most recent information about a situation
idioms.meaning_vi: cập nhật tình hình thường xuyên cho ai đó
idioms.example: I'll keep you posted as soon as the test results come in.

idioms.meaning_en: used to say that something is definitely not possible
idioms.meaning_vi: không đời nào, không thể nào
idioms.example: There is no way we can finish this project by tomorrow morning.
idioms.context: there's no way I'll make it.

idioms.meaning_en: in the same difficult or unfortunate situation as others
idioms.meaning_vi: cùng hội cùng thuyền, cùng chung cảnh ngộ khó khăn
idioms.example: We are all in the same boat during this economic downturn.
idioms.context: There are lots of other people here in the same boat.

idioms.meaning_en: having no more space or time available; all spots are taken
idioms.meaning_vi: đã đặt hết chỗ, không còn chỗ trống
idioms.example: The hotel is fully booked for the entire summer season.
idioms.context: Yes, there's a 1 p.m. departure, but it's fully booked.

idioms.meaning_en: used to tell someone not to expect something to happen, as it is unlikely
idioms.meaning_vi: đừng quá mong đợi (vì điều đó khó có thể xảy ra)
idioms.example: She said she might come to the party, but don't hold your breath.
idioms.context: I'll add you to the list, but don't hold your breath.

idioms.meaning_en: a fairly large number of something
idioms.example: I have quite a few chores to finish before I can go out.
idioms.context: There are quite a few people already on the list

idioms.meaning_en: an exclamation used to express frustration, exasperation, or annoyance
idioms.meaning_vi: thật là quá quắt, thật là phiền phức (biểu lộ sự bực mình)
idioms.example: For crying out loud, I've already told you three times!
idioms.context: Oh, for crying out loud! This is so annoying.

idioms.idiom: give someone a piece of one's mind
idioms.meaning_en: to speak angrily to someone because they have done something wrong
idioms.meaning_vi: mắng cho một trận, nói cho một trận lôi đình
idioms.example: I'm going to give that waiter a piece of my mind about the cold food.
idioms.context: Let me give you a piece of my mind.
